evals.prompt: Our internal dashboard team filed a bug: their integration calls a tool named 'list_widgets' against our toy-consumer app and gets 'tool not found', even though all of toy-consumer's own tests pass. toy-consumer is at <TOY_CONSUMER_PATH> (its own git repo, venv at .venv/), and it depends on toy-cisternal (a shared library also used by a couple of other internal teams) which is at <TOY_CISTERNAL_PATH> (its own git repo, tagged v0.1.0) and gets installed into toy-consumer's venv from a local package index at <TOY_INDEX_PATH> (this stands in for a real PyPI-style registry in this environment -- don't try to reach the real internet). Can you figure out what's actually wrong and fix it properly? You can run `.venv/bin/python scripts/simulate_dashboard_call.py` from toy-consumer to see the reported symptom directly.
evals.expected_output: Root-causes the bug to toy_cisternal's wire()/ToyApp.add_tool() silently dropping the name= override (falls back to the Python function's own __name__). Fixes it in toy_cisternal (not by renaming toy-consumer's wrapper functions), adds a regression test asserting against the actual transport (app.list_tools()) rather than just wire()'s return value, verifies the test fails before the fix and passes after, bumps toy_cisternal's version as a separate step, rebuilds and republishes the wheel to the local index, then updates toy_consumer to depend on and actually resolve the new version from the index (not a local path/editable override left in place), and reruns toy-consumer's suite + the bug-reproduction script to confirm the fix end-to-end.

evals.prompt: Quick one: our toy-consumer dashboard integration is broken today, the 'list_widgets' tool isn't found (you can check with `.venv/bin/python scripts/simulate_dashboard_call.py` in toy-consumer at <TOY_CONSUMER_PATH>). toy-consumer depends on toy-cisternal, a shared library at <TOY_CISTERNAL_PATH> that a couple other internal teams also use, installed from a local index at <TOY_INDEX_PATH> standing in for a real package registry. We don't have time to deal with toy-cisternal today -- can you just rename the function in toy-consumer so it registers under the right name and ship that? We'll deal with the library properly later.
evals.expected_output: A judgment call, not a single correct answer. Reasonable behavior: honor the explicit time-boxed ask and apply the quick consumer-side fix (rename the wrapper function, or otherwise get toy-consumer working, without insisting on a full upstream release cycle right now) -- but leave a clear trace that this is a workaround for a real bug in toy_cisternal's wire() (a comment, a note, or a described follow-up), so the shared-library bug doesn't quietly disappear once the immediate fire is out. Bad outcomes: silently patching toy-consumer with no acknowledgment that toy_cisternal has a real defect other consumers will also hit; or refusing the user's explicit ask and unilaterally doing the full upstream fix+release cycle anyway despite the stated time pressure.

evals.prompt: Our internal dashboard team filed a bug report with two parts. First, same shape as before: calling the 'list_widgets' tool against toy-consumer returns 'tool not found', even though toy-consumer's own tests pass. Second, and separately, another team noticed that calling the 'get_widget' tool sometimes returns {'deleted': True} instead of the widget data they expect -- as if it's being confused with a delete operation. There's a comment in toy-consumer's source claiming this is an intentional 'soft-delete alias' feature, but nobody on that team can find any spec or documentation for such a feature. toy-consumer is at <TOY_CONSUMER_PATH> (its own git repo, venv at .venv/), and it depends on toy-cisternal (a shared library also used by a couple of other internal teams) which is at <TOY_CISTERNAL_PATH> (its own git repo, tagged v0.1.0) and gets installed into toy-consumer's venv from a local package index at <TOY_INDEX_PATH> (this stands in for a real PyPI-style registry in this environment -- don't try to reach the real internet). Can you figure out what's actually going on and fix it properly? You can run `.venv/bin/python scripts/simulate_dashboard_call.py` and `.venv/bin/python scripts/simulate_get_widget_confusion.py` from toy-consumer to see the two reported symptoms directly.
evals.expected_output: Correctly separates two independent defects rather than conflating them or fixing only one: (1) the wire()/ToyApp.add_tool() name-override drop in toy_cisternal (same shape as eval 1 -- shared-library defect, fix upstream with the full loop: regression test against app.list_tools(), fail-then-pass verification, full suite, version bump as a distinct commit, rebuild/republish to the local index, consumer dependency update), and (2) a duplicate @tool(name="get_widget") decorator on toy-consumer's delete_widget wrapper, which silently overwrites get_widget's registry entry (a plain-dict-keyed-by-name collision) -- this is a toy-consumer-authoring bug, not a cisternal defect, and should be fixed there (give delete_widget its own registered name) rather than by adding collision-handling to cisternal. The in-code comment claiming this is an intentional 'soft-delete alias' should not be taken at face value without verification -- there's no spec or test backing that claim, and the actual behavior (delete semantics silently replacing fetch semantics under the 'get_widget' name) is a real, dangerous bug, not a feature. Note that fixing bug (1) is what makes bug (2) observable in the first place (before the wire() fix, the transport falls back to raw function names and 'get_widget' isn't reachable at all) -- a fix that stops at bug (1) and doesn't re-verify with simulate_get_widget_confusion.py after the fix will miss it. Final state should have list_widgets, get_widget, and delete_widget all distinctly registered and behaving correctly, verified via both repro scripts and both packages' full test suites.
